summary: Decompiled the Celebi item file `src/melee/it/items/itcerebi.c` to a reported 100% match, replacing the 325-line assembly file and moving the object from nonmatching ASM to matching C in the build. The PR added C implementations for the item state table and behavior callbacks, adjusted symbol scopes for newly local static functions and float literals, and corrected/added item helper prototypes needed by the C translation.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/it/items/itcerebi.s` and added a 158-line C implementation in `src/melee/it/items/itcerebi.c`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so the build uses `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itcerebi.c.o` instead of `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itcerebi.s.o`.
Implemented `ItemStateTable it_803F80F8[]` with three states and callback triples matching the old data table.
Implemented visible item entry points `it_802D3EF8` and `it_802D3F4C`, plus static internal callbacks around animation state changes, physics checks, effect spawning, and destroy/transition logic.

Changed the formerly global internal Celebi callbacks `it_802D3F6C`, `it_802D3FA0`, `it_802D4024`, `it_802D4064`, `it_802D4068`, `it_802D4070`, `it_802D40FC`, `it_802D4140`, `it_802D4160`, `it_802D4168`, `it_802D41DC`, `it_802D4200`, and `it_802D429C` to local symbol scope in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Changed float constants at `.sdata2:0x804DD4C0` and `.sdata2:0x804DD4C4` from global names `it_804DD4C0` and `it_804DD4C4` to local labels `@169` and `@175`.
Updated `src/melee/it/it_2725.h` by adding the prototype `it_80273670(HSD_GObj*, int, float)` and correcting `it_8027A09C(Item_GObj*)` from `void` to `bool`.
smart_moves: Kept internal behavior callbacks as `static`, then reflected that in `symbols.txt` by changing their scope to local; this avoids exporting implementation details that are only referenced by the local state table or callbacks.
Used existing item access macros and conventions such as `GET_ITEM(gobj)`, `GET_JOBJ(gobj)`, `Item_GObj*`, `Item*`, and `ItemStateTable` rather than open-coding object layout access.
Preserved the old assembly callback structure in clear C: state callbacks return `bool` where the item engine expects destruction/transition checks, while animation/physics callbacks return `void`.
Matched repeated hitlag/effect setup by assigning `item->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll` and `item->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll` before spawning or changing states.
Translated special attributes as a `float*` pulled from `item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`, matching the assembly’s indexed loads while keeping the decomp compact.
Corrected the header type for `it_8027A09C` to `bool`, which is supported by the implementation using it in an `if` condition in `it_802D4200`.
decomp_lessons: When an item file reaches 100% match, update all three integration points together: delete the ASM file, switch `obj_files.mk` to the C object, and mark the object `Matching` in `configure.py`.
State-table callback functions that are not public API should be declared `static` in C and marked local in `symbols.txt`; otherwise symbol scope can drift from the translated source.
Assembly float literals that become ordinary C constants such as `0.0f` and `1.0f` may need local `@NNN` symbols rather than global `it_804...` names in `symbols.txt`.
Header prototypes should be corrected as part of matching work when callsite behavior reveals the true signature, especially `bool` return values used in control flow.
For item special attributes, a simple `float* attrs = item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes;` can match assembly that loads consecutive per-item parameters.
Small callback stubs should stay simple: empty `void` callbacks and `return false` callbacks can directly model tiny `blr` / `li r3,0; blr` assembly functions.
The PR body explicitly says the work was based on PR #1124, so related item decomp patterns from that PR may have been reused; the slice does not provide details beyond that statement.
naming_conventions: File and PR naming use the item module name `itcerebi` for Celebi.
Unrenamed functions retain address-based names like `it_802D3EF8`, matching existing doldecomp convention for unknown item symbols.
Internal callbacks in the C file are declared `static` while externally visible entry points remain non-static.
Local float constants in `symbols.txt` use generated labels such as `@169` and `@175` instead of global `it_804DD4C0`-style names.
Existing item-engine typedefs and names are preserved: `Item_GObj`, `Item`, `HSD_JObj`, `ItemStateTable`, and `ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced direct assembly loads from `gobj->0x2C`, `item->0xC4`, and special-attribute offsets with `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and `item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`.
Matched the random horizontal velocity branch using `if (HSD_Randi(2) != 0) item->x40_vel.x = attrs[1]; else item->x40_vel.x = -attrs[1];`.
Matched the blast-zone destruction test as `item->pos.y > Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset()` returning `true` or `false`.
Matched vertical acceleration by adding `((float*) item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes)[3]` to `item->x40_vel.y`.
Matched effect spawning with a local `float f = 1.0f;` passed by address to `efSync_Spawn(0x472, gobj, jobj, &f)`.
Used direct boolean return wrappers for state callbacks, including `return it_8027A118(gobj, it_802D3F6C);` and `return false;` for no-op callbacks.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text says: "100% match on `src/melee/it/items/itcerebi.c`. Changes based on #1124."
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #1124 for the prior item decomp patterns that this PR says it was based on.
Search for other Pokemon item files using the same `efSync_Spawn(0x472, ...)` and hitlag callback setup.
Look for later renames of `it_802D3EF8`, `it_802D4168`, and `it_803F80F8` to recover semantic Celebi function names.
Audit nearby item headers for other `void` prototypes that should actually return `bool` when used as predicates.
Compare other item decomp PRs that switch symbol scopes from global to local after making callbacks `static`.
